name: Sendinês (Mirandese meridional)
notes: Sendinês subdialect of Mirandese, spoken in Vila de Sendim (southern extreme of Terra de Miranda). It inherits the full Central Mirandese system (graphemes_base=mwl: apical ⟨s⟩ /s̺ z̺/ vs predorsal ⟨c,ç,z⟩ /s z/, ⟨x⟩ /ʃ/, ⟨j,g⟩ /ʒ/, ⟨ch⟩ /tʃ/, ⟨nh⟩ /ɲ/, nasal-vowel digraphs) and overrides only the two documented Sendinês deltas: (1) monophthongisation of the Leonese diphthongs ⟨iê/ie⟩ → /i/ and ⟨uô/uo⟩ → /u/ (tirra not tiêrra, curpo not cuôrpo) — the Central/Raiano [je]/[wo] is reduced to [i]/[u]; (2) DEPALATALISATION of /ʎ/ → /l/: words that other dialects say with ⟨lh⟩ /ʎ/ are said with /l/ (alá for alhá, lhado→lado, lhuç→[ˈlus], lhobo→[ˈlobu]), and word-initial ⟨l⟩ is likewise plain /l/ (no Leonese initial palatalisation). The Primeiro Aditamento à Convenção (February 2000) permits Sendinese writers to spell word-initial ⟨l-⟩ (not ⟨lh-⟩). Because ⟨lh⟩ → /l/ is overridden here, the Central word-initial ⟨l⟩ → /ʎ/ rule is also cancelled via the positional override (l stays /l/ in every position).

sources.author: Ferreira, M.B. & Raposo, D. (coords.)
sources.title: Convenção Ortográfica da Língua Mirandesa
sources.publisher: Câmara Municipal de Miranda do Douro / Centro de Linguística da Universidade de Lisboa
sources.notes: Official orthography, base document for all Mirandese variants.

sources.author: Ferreira, M.B. & Raposo, D. (coords.)
sources.title: Primeiro Aditamento à Convenção Ortográfica da Língua Mirandesa
sources.publisher: Câmara Municipal de Miranda do Douro / Centro de Linguística da Universidade de Lisboa
sources.notes: February 2000 addendum. Codifies Sendinese l- (not lh-) word-initially; Sendinese ie/uo (no accent) vs central iê/uô. Source: secondary (standalone PDF not located).

stress.notes: Convenção Ortográfica da Língua Mirandesa (1999) accentuation, Portuguese-based: paroxytone default; written accent (acute or tilde) overrides. Oxytone-attracting endings reflect Mirandese orthography: consonant-final r/l/z/ç (rapaç, Pertual, falar) and the final nasals written with -n in the Asturleonese manner — -in (camin, Sendin), -un, and the systematically oxytone -on class from Latin -ōnem (naçon, coraçon, Associaçon). Unmarked -an/-en verb endings stay paroxytone as in Portuguese -am/-em. Dialect mwl-x-sendim: no evidence of stress-pattern divergence from standard Mirandese; block copied from mwl (cliticless_words included, since stress is not inherited). Sendinês keeps the plain [l] for the article and depalatalises ⟨lh⟩/initial-⟨l⟩ to [l] by its own positional table, and monophthongises ⟨ie⟩→[i] so 'quien' → [ˈkĩ]; it therefore needs no word_exceptions overriding the mwl parent's.

location.notes: Sendim — the southern Terra de Miranda village the sub-dialect is named for. [Wikidata Q2121828 P625]
